title: Our Changing Earth
passage:
Our Changing Earth

Our Earth is getting warmer every year. This rise in temperature is called global warming. Why is this happening? It is happening because of human activities. People burn coal, oil, and gas in big factories to make electricity. We also use petrol to run cars and buses. These activities make greenhouse gases. These gases go up into the sky and cover the Earth like a big blanket. They trap the heat from the sun. This heat cannot go back into space, so our planet gets hotter. This change in the weather and temperature is called climate change.

Climate change causes many big problems for our world. First, the weather is changing. Some places have very hot summer days with no rain. Other places have heavy rain and big floods. Second, ice is melting in cold places. In the Arctic, the ice is melting very fast. When the ice melts, it becomes water. This water goes into the oceans. The sea level rises, and cities near the sea might flood in the future. Third, many animals are in danger. For example, polar bears live on sea ice. When the ice melts, polar bears lose their homes and cannot find food easily.

However, we can help our planet. We can do small things every day to save energy. We can turn off the lights and TVs when we leave a room. This saves electricity. We can ride bicycles, walk, or use buses instead of driving private cars. We can also recycle paper, plastic, and glass. Finally, we can plant more trees. Trees are very important because they clean the air and take in greenhouse gases. If we all work together, we can protect our beautiful Earth.

questions.explanation: The text states: 'Why is this happening? It is happening because of human activities.'

questions.explanation: The text states: 'In the Arctic, the ice is melting very fast.'

vocabulary.meaning: the increase in the temperature of the Earth
gases that trap heat in the air and make the Earth warmer
to change from solid ice into liquid water because of heat
a large amount of water covering an area that is usually dry
to keep someone or something safe from danger or damage

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tips)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ A1-A2, các câu hỏi thường sử dụng từ vựng giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy tìm các từ khóa nổi bật như 'blanket', 'Arctic', 'electricity' để định vị thông tin nhanh nhất.

2. Lưu ý giới hạn từ: Với dạng bài Sentence Completion, hãy chú ý yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS' (Không quá 2 từ). Bạn chỉ cần chép chính xác từ trong bài, không cần tự thay đổi dạng từ.

3. Phân biệt FALSE và NOT GIVEN: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi ngược hoàn toàn với bài đọc, chọn FALSE. Nếu bài đọc không nhắc đến thông tin đó (ví dụ: gấu Bắc Cực thích ăn gì), chọn NOT GIVEN.
